title: I Built an AI Memory Agent That Forgets on Purpose — Then Spent Two Days Proving It Actually Works
excerpt: Every "AI memory agent" I looked at before starting this does the same trick: embed every message,...
content_html:
Every "AI memory agent" I looked at before starting this does the same trick: embed every message, dump it in a vector database, retrieve the top-k most similar chunks at query time. That's not memory. That's a search index. It never forgets anything, it treats "nice weather today" with the same weight as "I'm allergic to penicillin," and it gets slower and dumber the longer it runs because retrieval gets noisier with every near-duplicate you never clean up.

For the Global AI Hackathon Series with Qwen Cloud (Track 1: MemoryAgent), the brief asked for three specific things: efficient storage and retrieval, timely forgetting of outdated information, and recalling critical memories within a limited context window. That middle one is the one almost nobody builds, because naive vector storage has no concept of "outdated." So I built it — real decay math, real consolidation, real contradiction detection — and then benchmarked it against the naive approach to prove it actually works, instead of just claiming it does.

This is the story of building that, on Qwen Cloud, in about two days, including the parts that broke.

The actual mechanism

Every memory Synapse stores gets a salience score that changes over time:

salience(t) = importance_score * recall_boost(recall_count) * exp(-lambda * hours_since_last_recall)
recall_boost(n) = 1 + log(1 + n)
lambda = ln(2) / half_life_hours

Two things matter here. First, importance_score isn't a keyword heuristic — it's a real structured-output call to Qwen at write time, scoring the memory on explicit signals ("remember this"), decision-relevance (is this the kind of fact that should change future behavior?), and specificity. Second, the half-life isn't a single global constant. Episodic details — what you mentioned on a random Tuesday — decay in about 72 hours unless reinforced. Semantic facts — stable preferences, "I'm vegetarian" — decay over 30 days. That asymmetry is the whole point: the system should forget what you talked about, not what you told it matters.

On top of decay, a background "sleep pass" does two more things a naive vector store can't:

  1. Consolidation — clusters repeated episodic mentions (five separate messages about "working on my hackathon project") into one semantic memory, retiring the originals.
  2. Contradiction detection — when a new fact directly supersedes an old one (you say you live in Berlin, then three weeks later you say you just moved to Lisbon), Qwen judges the pair and retires the stale one. This is the literal "timely forgetting of outdated information" the brief asks for.

Building it on Qwen Cloud

Every LLM call in this project — chat replies, importance scoring, memory extraction, contradiction judgment, cluster summarization, and even the benchmark's own LLM-as-judge scoring — goes to a real Qwen Cloud endpoint. No mocked responses, anywhere, including in the benchmark. That was a hard rule I set for myself: if a mechanism didn't work for real, I'd cut the claim rather than fake the output.

Getting there wasn't instant. Qwen Cloud's OpenAI-compatible endpoint is workspace-specific — not the generic host you'd expect, but a ws-<workspace-id>.<region>.maas.aliyuncs.com URL you have to find on your own console's API-key page. Past that, qwen-max/qwen3.7-plus handled chat, scoring, extraction, and consolidation, and text-embedding-v3 handled every embedding call for retrieval and clustering.

One thing that genuinely surprised me: switching to a faster chat model (qwen3.6-flash) to speed up the benchmark run occasionally returned malformed JSON on structured-output calls — an empty {} where a real score should've been. Rather than paper over it with a fallback default, I built a retry wrapper that specifically re-prompts on shape failure, not just network failure:

def _chat_json_retrying(system_prompt, user_prompt, validate, temperature=0.2, max_attempts=3):
    for attempt in range(max_attempts):
        result = _chat_json(system_prompt, user_prompt, temperature)
        if validate(result):
            return result
    raise ValueError("Qwen returned an invalid shape after retries")

Small thing, but it's the difference between a benchmark that silently produces garbage numbers and one that fails loudly when something's actually wrong.

The bug that almost invalidated the whole benchmark

Here's the one I'm most honest about. The consolidation pass is supposed to compare timestamps to figure out which of two contradicting memories is newer. I ran the full benchmark — 110 turns across a simulated 40-day conversation — using a fabricated now value passed through the code so I could compress 40 days into one script run instead of waiting 40 real days.

Except one function wasn't using that fabricated time. It was quietly falling back to datetime.now() — real wall-clock time — when deciding which memory was "newer." Every memory in the simulation, regardless of its fictional in-conversation date, actually got written at roughly the same real moment. So the timestamp comparison the contradiction check depended on was comparing noise, not the actual simulated chronology.

I found it by directly querying the database after the benchmark run showed a backwards result — the system confidently answering with a stale fact instead of the current one. Not a vague "accuracy could be better," a specific, traceable bug. I fixed it by threading the simulated now explicitly through every function that touches timestamps, instead of letting anything default to real time, and wrote a regression test that reproduces the exact failure:

def test_stale_fact_loses_even_when_inserted_after_correct_one():
    # The correct fact is "older" in simulated time but inserted into the DB
    # *after* the stale one — proving the fix isn't relying on insertion order.
    ...

The numbers, honestly

I built a second agent — same Qwen models, same embeddings, the only difference being zero decay, zero consolidation, zero pruning — and ran both through the identical conversation. Real results:

  • Memory count: Synapse plateaus around 117 active memories; the naive baseline grows almost linearly to 220.
  • Token cost per query: Synapse stays flat in the 130–170 range for the entire run; the naive baseline spikes past 2000 tokens as the index grows.
  • Recall accuracy: Synapse scored 71% against the naive baseline's 95% — and I'm not hiding that number.

That last one is worth sitting with. I could have cherry-picked the two metrics that make the project look great and left it there. Instead I dug into why Synapse lost on raw recall, and found two specific, fixable causes: a similarity pre-filter gate that was rejecting a real contradiction phrased in different words (measured similarity as low as 0.59 against a 0.75 gate), and a re-ranking formula where a frequently-recalled generic fact could out-rank a less-reinforced but more relevant one. I fixed both, verified the fixes with targeted regression tests and a live end-to-end test against the deployed app, and documented all of it — including that I didn't have time to re-run the full multi-hour benchmark against the fixed code before the deadline.

A chart you can trust is worth more than a chart that flatters you.

What I'd tell someone starting this track

  • If your memory mechanism only gets exercised at small scale, you will not find your real bugs. Mine only surfaced at 100+ turns, when timestamps and salience actually had room to diverge.
  • Build the naive baseline first, or at least in parallel. Having something to diff against turns "trust me it's smart" into a number.
  • When something breaks late in a long-running job, resist the instinct to patch around it silently. My retry-on-invalid-shape wrapper and my rollback-and-continue error handling in the benchmark loop both came from refusing to let one bad LLM response take down a two-hour run.

Synapse is live on Alibaba Cloud ECS, the code is public and MIT-licensed, and the full honest writeup — including the bugs, the fixes, and the numbers before and after — is in the repo.

Repo: github.com/Boweii22/Synapse
Live demo: http://8.208.98.93
Demo video: youtu.be/0SXzcWqlZog

Built for Track 1 (MemoryAgent) of the Global AI Hackathon Series with Qwen Cloud.

blocks.html_en: Every "AI memory agent" I looked at before starting this does the same trick: embed every message, dump it in a vector database, retrieve the top-k most similar chunks at query time. That's not memory. That's a search index. It never forgets anything, it treats "nice weather today" with the same weight as "I'm allergic to penicillin," and it gets slower and dumber the longer it runs because retrieval gets noisier with every near-duplicate you never clean up.

sentences.en: Every "AI memory agent" I looked at before starting this does the same trick: embed every message, dump it in a vector database, retrieve the top-k most similar chunks at query time.
sentences.zh: 在开始之前我查看的每个“AI 内存代理”都执行相同的技巧：嵌入每条消息，将其转储到向量数据库中，在查询时检索前 k 个最相似的块。

sentences.en: That's not memory.
sentences.zh: 那不是记忆。

sentences.en: That's a search index.
sentences.zh: 那是一个搜索索引。

sentences.en: It never forgets anything, it treats "nice weather today" with the same weight as "I'm allergic to penicillin," and it gets slower and dumber the longer it runs because retrieval gets noisier with every near-duplicate you never clean up.
sentences.zh: 它永远不会忘记任何事情，它对待“今天的好天气”的重要性与“我对青霉素过敏”的重要性相同，而且它运行的时间越长，它就会变得越慢和愚蠢，因为每一个你从未清理过的近乎重复的检索都会变得更加嘈杂。

blocks.html_en: For the Global AI Hackathon Series with Qwen Cloud (Track 1: MemoryAgent), the brief asked for three specific things: efficient storage and retrieval, timely forgetting of outdated information, and recalling critical memories within a limited context window. That middle one is the one almost nobody builds, because naive vector storage has no concept of "outdated." So I built it — real decay math, real consolidation, real contradiction detection — and then benchmarked it against the naive approach to prove it actually works, instead of just claiming it does.

sentences.en: For the Global AI Hackathon Series with Qwen Cloud (Track 1: MemoryAgent), the brief asked for three specific things: efficient storage and retrieval, timely forgetting of outdated information, and recalling critical memories within a limited context window.
sentences.zh: 对于 Qwen Cloud 的全球 AI 黑客马拉松系列（赛道 1：MemoryAgent），简报要求实现三个具体目标：高效存储和检索、及时忘记过时信息以及在有限的上下文窗口内回忆关键记忆。

sentences.en: That middle one is the one almost nobody builds, because naive vector storage has no concept of "outdated."
sentences.zh: 中间的那个几乎没有人构建，因为天真的矢量存储没有“过时”的概念。

sentences.en: So I built it — real decay math, real consolidation, real contradiction detection — and then benchmarked it against the naive approach to prove it actually works, instead of just claiming it does.
sentences.zh: 所以我构建了它——真正的衰变数学、真正的合并、真正的矛盾检测——然后将它与天真的方法进行基准测试，以证明它确实有效，而不是仅仅声称它有效。

blocks.html_en: This is the story of building that, on Qwen Cloud, in about two days, including the parts that broke.

sentences.en: This is the story of building that, on Qwen Cloud, in about two days, including the parts that broke.
sentences.zh: 这是在 Qwen Cloud 上花费大约两天时间构建的故事，包括损坏的部件。

blocks.html_en: The actual mechanism

sentences.en: The actual mechanism
sentences.zh: 实际机制

blocks.html_en: Every memory Synapse stores gets a salience score that changes over time:

sentences.en: Every memory Synapse stores gets a salience score that changes over time:
sentences.zh: Synapse 存储的每个内存都会获得一个随时间变化的显着性分数：

sentences.en: Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode
sentences.zh: 进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

blocks.html_en: Two things matter here. First, importance_score isn't a keyword heuristic — it's a real structured-output call to Qwen at write time, scoring the memory on explicit signals ("remember this"), decision-relevance (is this the kind of fact that should change future behavior?), and specificity. Second, the half-life isn't a single global constant. Episodic details — what you mentioned on a random Tuesday — decay in about 72 hours unless reinforced. Semantic facts — stable preferences, "I'm vegetarian" — decay over 30 days. That asymmetry is the whole point: the system should forget what you talked about, not what you told it matters.

sentences.en: Two things matter here.
sentences.zh: 这里有两件事很重要。

sentences.en: First, importance_score isn't a keyword heuristic — it's a real structured-output call to Qwen at write time, scoring the memory on explicit signals ("remember this"), decision-relevance (is this the kind of fact that should change future behavior?), and specificity.
sentences.zh: 首先，importance_score 不是一个关键字启发式方法——它是在写入时对 Qwen 的真正结构化输出调用，根据显式信号（“记住这一点”）、决策相关性（这是应该改变未来行为的事实吗？）和特异性对记忆进行评分。

sentences.en: Second, the half-life isn't a single global constant.
sentences.zh: 其次，半衰期不是一个单一的全局常数。

sentences.en: Episodic details — what you mentioned on a random Tuesday — decay in about 72 hours unless reinforced.
sentences.zh: 情景细节——你在周二随机提到的——除非得到强化，否则会在大约 72 小时内消失。

sentences.en: Semantic facts — stable preferences, "I'm vegetarian" — decay over 30 days.
sentences.zh: 语义事实——稳定的偏好，“我是素食主义者”——会在 30 天内衰减。

sentences.en: That asymmetry is the whole point: the system should forget what you talked about, not what you told it matters.
sentences.zh: 这种不对称就是重点：系统应该忘记你谈论的内容，而不是你告诉它的内容。

blocks.html_en: On top of decay, a background "sleep pass" does two more things a naive vector store can't:

sentences.en: On top of decay, a background "sleep pass" does two more things a naive vector store can't:
sentences.zh: 除了衰减之外，背景“睡眠通行证”还可以做两件事，这是简单的向量存储无法做到的：

blocks.html_en: • Consolidation — clusters repeated episodic mentions (five separate messages about "working on my hackathon project") into one semantic memory, retiring the originals.

sentences.en: Consolidation — clusters repeated episodic mentions (five separate messages about "working on my hackathon project") into one semantic memory, retiring the originals.
sentences.zh: 巩固——将重复的片段提及（关于“从事我的黑客马拉松项目”的五个单独的消息）聚集到一个语义记忆中，淘汰原来的内容。

blocks.html_en: • Contradiction detection — when a new fact directly supersedes an old one (you say you live in Berlin, then three weeks later you say you just moved to Lisbon), Qwen judges the pair and retires the stale one. This is the literal "timely forgetting of outdated information" the brief asks for.

sentences.en: Contradiction detection — when a new fact directly supersedes an old one (you say you live in Berlin, then three weeks later you say you just moved to Lisbon), Qwen judges the pair and retires the stale one.
sentences.zh: 矛盾检测——当一个新事实直接取代一个旧事实时（你说你住在柏林，三周后你说你刚搬到里斯本），Qwen 会判断这对事实并淘汰陈旧的事实。

sentences.en: This is the literal "timely forgetting of outdated information" the brief asks for.
sentences.zh: 这就是简报所要求的“及时忘记过时的信息”。

blocks.html_en: Building it on Qwen Cloud

sentences.en: Building it on Qwen Cloud
sentences.zh: 在 Qwen Cloud 上构建

blocks.html_en: Every LLM call in this project — chat replies, importance scoring, memory extraction, contradiction judgment, cluster summarization, and even the benchmark's own LLM-as-judge scoring — goes to a real Qwen Cloud endpoint. No mocked responses, anywhere, including in the benchmark. That was a hard rule I set for myself: if a mechanism didn't work for real, I'd cut the claim rather than fake the output.

sentences.en: Every LLM call in this project — chat replies, importance scoring, memory extraction, contradiction judgment, cluster summarization, and even the benchmark's own LLM-as-judge scoring — goes to a real Qwen Cloud endpoint.
sentences.zh: 该项目中的每个 LLM 调用（聊天回复、重要性评分、内存提取、矛盾判断、聚类摘要，甚至基准测试自己的 LLM 作为评判评分）都会发送到真正的 Qwen Cloud 端点。

sentences.en: No mocked responses, anywhere, including in the benchmark.
sentences.zh: 在任何地方都没有嘲笑的回应，包括在基准测试中。

sentences.en: That was a hard rule I set for myself: if a mechanism didn't work for real, I'd cut the claim rather than fake the output.
sentences.zh: 这是我为自己设定的一条硬性规则：如果一种机制不能真正发挥作用，我会取消声明，而不是伪造输出。

blocks.html_en: Getting there wasn't instant. Qwen Cloud's OpenAI-compatible endpoint is workspace-specific — not the generic host you'd expect, but a ws-<workspace-id>.<region>.maas.aliyuncs.com URL you have to find on your own console's API-key page. Past that, qwen-max/qwen3.7-plus handled chat, scoring, extraction, and consolidation, and text-embedding-v3 handled every embedding call for retrieval and clustering.

sentences.zh: 到达那里并不是即时的。

sentences.en: Qwen Cloud's OpenAI-compatible endpoint is workspace-specific — not the generic host you'd expect, but a ws-<workspace-id>.<region>.maas.aliyuncs.com URL you have to find on your own console's API-key page.

sentences.en: Past that, qwen-max/qwen3.7-plus handled chat, scoring, extraction, and consolidation, and text-embedding-v3 handled every embedding call for retrieval and clustering.
sentences.zh: 除此之外，qwen-max/qwen3.7-plus 处理聊天、评分、提取和合并，而 text-embedding-v3 处理用于检索和聚类的每个嵌入调用。

sentences.zh: 我没有用后备默认值来覆盖它，而是构建了一个重试包装器，专门重新提示形状故障，而不仅仅是网络故障：

sentences.en: The consolidation pass is supposed to compare timestamps to figure out which of two contradicting memories is newer.
sentences.zh: 合并过程应该比较时间戳，以找出两个相互矛盾的记忆中哪一个更新。

sentences.en: I ran the full benchmark — 110 turns across a simulated 40-day conversation — using a fabricated now value passed through the code so I could compress 40 days into one script run instead of waiting 40 real days.
sentences.zh: 我运行了完整的基准测试——模拟 40 天的对话，进行了 110 次对话——使用通过代码传递的虚构现在值，这样我就可以将 40 天压缩到一个脚本运行中，而不是等待 40 天。

blocks.html_en: Except one function wasn't using that fabricated time. It was quietly falling back to datetime.now() — real wall-clock time — when deciding which memory was "newer." Every memory in the simulation, regardless of its fictional in-conversation date, actually got written at roughly the same real moment. So the timestamp comparison the contradiction check depended on was comparing noise, not the actual simulated chronology.

sentences.en: Except one function wasn't using that fabricated time.
sentences.zh: 除了一个函数没有使用那个虚构的时间。

sentences.en: It was quietly falling back to datetime.now() — real wall-clock time — when deciding which memory was "newer."
sentences.zh: 在决定哪个内存“较新”时，它会悄悄地回到 datetime.now() — 真正的挂钟时间。

sentences.en: Every memory in the simulation, regardless of its fictional in-conversation date, actually got written at roughly the same real moment.
sentences.zh: 模拟中的每条记忆，无论其虚构的对话日期如何，实际上都是在大致相同的真实时刻写入的。

sentences.en: So the timestamp comparison the contradiction check depended on was comparing noise, not the actual simulated chronology.
sentences.zh: 因此，矛盾检查所依赖的时间戳比较是比较噪声，而不是实际模拟的时间顺序。

blocks.html_en: I found it by directly querying the database after the benchmark run showed a backwards result — the system confidently answering with a stale fact instead of the current one. Not a vague "accuracy could be better," a specific, traceable bug. I fixed it by threading the simulated now explicitly through every function that touches timestamps, instead of letting anything default to real time, and wrote a regression test that reproduces the exact failure:

sentences.en: I found it by directly querying the database after the benchmark run showed a backwards result — the system confidently answering with a stale fact instead of the current one.
sentences.zh: 我通过在基准测试运行显示向后结果后直接查询数据库找到了它——系统自信地回答了一个过时的事实而不是当前的事实。

sentences.en: Not a vague "accuracy could be better," a specific, traceable bug.
sentences.zh: 不是一个模糊的“准确性可以更好”，而是一个具体的、可追踪的错误。

sentences.en: I fixed it by threading the simulated now explicitly through every function that touches timestamps, instead of letting anything default to real time, and wrote a regression test that reproduces the exact failure:
sentences.zh: 我通过将模拟现在明确地线程化通过每个涉及时间戳的函数来修复它，而不是让任何东西默认为实时，并编写了一个回归测试来重现确切的失败：

sentences.en: Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode
sentences.zh: 进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

blocks.html_en: The numbers, honestly

sentences.en: The numbers, honestly
sentences.zh: 老实说，数字

blocks.html_en: I built a second agent — same Qwen models, same embeddings, the only difference being zero decay, zero consolidation, zero pruning — and ran both through the identical conversation. Real results:

sentences.en: I built a second agent — same Qwen models, same embeddings, the only difference being zero decay, zero consolidation, zero pruning — and ran both through the identical conversation.
sentences.zh: 我构建了第二个代理——相同的 Qwen 模型、相同的嵌入，唯一的区别是零衰减、零合并、零修剪——并通过相同的对话运行这两个代理。

sentences.en: Real results:
sentences.zh: 真实结果：

blocks.html_en: • Memory count: Synapse plateaus around 117 active memories; the naive baseline grows almost linearly to 220.

sentences.en: Memory count: Synapse plateaus around 117 active memories; the naive baseline grows almost linearly to 220.
sentences.zh: 记忆数量：突触稳定在 117 个活跃记忆左右；朴素基线几乎线性增长到 220。

blocks.html_en: • Token cost per query: Synapse stays flat in the 130–170 range for the entire run; the naive baseline spikes past 2000 tokens as the index grows.

sentences.en: Token cost per query: Synapse stays flat in the 130–170 range for the entire run; the naive baseline spikes past 2000 tokens as the index grows.
sentences.zh: 每个查询的令牌成本：Synapse 在整个运行过程中保持在 130-170 范围内；随着指数的增长，初始基线飙升至 2000 个代币以上。

blocks.html_en: • Recall accuracy: Synapse scored 71% against the naive baseline's 95% — and I'm not hiding that number.

sentences.en: Recall accuracy: Synapse scored 71% against the naive baseline's 95% — and I'm not hiding that number.
sentences.zh: 回忆准确度：突触得分为 71%，而初始基线为 95%——而且我并没有隐瞒这个数字。

blocks.html_en: That last one is worth sitting with. I could have cherry-picked the two metrics that make the project look great and left it there. Instead I dug into why Synapse lost on raw recall, and found two specific, fixable causes: a similarity pre-filter gate that was rejecting a real contradiction phrased in different words (measured similarity as low as 0.59 against a 0.75 gate), and a re-ranking formula where a frequently-recalled generic fact could out-rank a less-reinforced but more relevant one. I fixed both, verified the fixes with targeted regression tests and a live end-to-end test against the deployed app, and documented all of it — including that I didn't have time to re-run the full multi-hour benchmark against the fixed code before the deadline.

sentences.en: That last one is worth sitting with.
sentences.zh: 最后一个值得一坐。

sentences.en: I could have cherry-picked the two metrics that make the project look great and left it there.
sentences.zh: 我本可以精心挑选两个使项目看起来很棒的指标，然后将其留在那里。

sentences.en: Instead I dug into why Synapse lost on raw recall, and found two specific, fixable causes: a similarity pre-filter gate that was rejecting a real contradiction phrased in different words (measured similarity as low as 0.59 against a 0.75 gate), and a re-ranking formula where a frequently-recalled generic fact could out-rank a less-reinforced but more relevant one.
sentences.zh: 相反，我深入研究了 Synapse 在原始回忆上失败的原因，并发现了两个具体的、可修复的原因：一个相似性预过滤门，它拒绝用不同单词表达的真正矛盾（相对于 0.75 的门，测量到的相似度低至 0.59）；以及一个重新排序公式，其中经常回忆的一般事实可能会超过强化程度较低但相关性更高的事实。

sentences.en: I fixed both, verified the fixes with targeted regression tests and a live end-to-end test against the deployed app, and documented all of it — including that I didn't have time to re-run the full multi-hour benchmark against the fixed code before the deadline.
sentences.zh: 我修复了这两个问题，通过有针对性的回归测试和针对已部署应用程序的实时端到端测试验证了修复，并记录了所有这些 - 包括我没有时间在截止日期之前针对固定代码重新运行完整的多小时基准测试。

blocks.html_en: A chart you can trust is worth more than a chart that flatters you.

sentences.en: A chart you can trust is worth more than a chart that flatters you.
sentences.zh: 一张值得您信赖的图表比一张让您奉承的图表更有价值。

blocks.html_en: What I'd tell someone starting this track

sentences.en: What I'd tell someone starting this track
sentences.zh: 我会告诉开始这首歌的人

blocks.html_en: • If your memory mechanism only gets exercised at small scale, you will not find your real bugs. Mine only surfaced at 100+ turns, when timestamps and salience actually had room to diverge.

sentences.en: If your memory mechanism only gets exercised at small scale, you will not find your real bugs.
sentences.zh: 如果你的记忆机制只在小范围内得到锻炼，你将无法发现真正的错误。

sentences.en: Mine only surfaced at 100+ turns, when timestamps and salience actually had room to diverge.
sentences.zh: 我的只有在 100 多个回合时才出现，此时时间戳和显着性实际上有分歧的空间。

blocks.html_en: • Build the naive baseline first, or at least in parallel. Having something to diff against turns "trust me it's smart" into a number.

sentences.en: Build the naive baseline first, or at least in parallel.
sentences.zh: 首先，或者至少并行地构建初始基线。

sentences.en: Having something to diff against turns "trust me it's smart" into a number.
sentences.zh: 有一些可以比较的东西将“相信我，这很聪明”变成了一个数字。

blocks.html_en: • When something breaks late in a long-running job, resist the instinct to patch around it silently. My retry-on-invalid-shape wrapper and my rollback-and-continue error handling in the benchmark loop both came from refusing to let one bad LLM response take down a two-hour run.

sentences.en: When something breaks late in a long-running job, resist the instinct to patch around it silently.
sentences.zh: 当长时间运行的工作中出现问题时，请抵制默默修补它的本能。

sentences.en: My retry-on-invalid-shape wrapper and my rollback-and-continue error handling in the benchmark loop both came from refusing to let one bad LLM response take down a two-hour run.
sentences.zh: 我的重试无效形状包装器和基准循环中的回滚并继续错误处理都来自于拒绝让一个错误的 LLM 响应导致两小时的运行中断。

blocks.html_en: Synapse is live on Alibaba Cloud ECS, the code is public and MIT-licensed, and the full honest writeup — including the bugs, the fixes, and the numbers before and after — is in the repo.

sentences.en: Synapse is live on Alibaba Cloud ECS, the code is public and MIT-licensed, and the full honest writeup — including the bugs, the fixes, and the numbers before and after — is in the repo.
sentences.zh: Synapse 已在阿里云 ECS 上运行，代码是公开的并获得 MIT 许可，完整的诚实记录（包括错误、修复以及前后的数字）位于存储库中。

blocks.html_en:
Repo: github.com/Boweii22/Synapse
Live demo: http://8.208.98.93
Demo video: youtu.be/0SXzcWqlZog

sentences.en: Repo: github.com/Boweii22/Synapse Live demo: http://8.208.98.93 Demo video: youtu.be/0SXzcWqlZog
sentences.zh: 仓库：github.com/Boweii22/Synapse 现场演示：http://8.208.98.93 演示视频：youtu.be/0SXzcWqlZog

blocks.html_en: Built for Track 1 (MemoryAgent) of the Global AI Hackathon Series with Qwen Cloud.

sentences.en: Built for Track 1 (MemoryAgent) of the Global AI Hackathon Series with Qwen Cloud.
sentences.zh: 借助 Qwen Cloud 专为全球 AI 黑客马拉松系列的第 1 场 (MemoryAgent) 打造。

content_html_zh:
在开始之前我查看的每个“AI 内存代理”都执行相同的技巧：嵌入每条消息，将其转储到向量数据库中，在查询时检索前 k 个最相似的块。 那不是记忆。 那是一个搜索索引。 它永远不会忘记任何事情，它对待“今天的好天气”的重要性与“我对青霉素过敏”的重要性相同，而且它运行的时间越长，它就会变得越慢和愚蠢，因为每一个你从未清理过的近乎重复的检索都会变得更加嘈杂。

对于 Qwen Cloud 的全球 AI 黑客马拉松系列（赛道 1：MemoryAgent），简报要求实现三个具体目标：高效存储和检索、及时忘记过时信息以及在有限的上下文窗口内回忆关键记忆。 中间的那个几乎没有人构建，因为天真的矢量存储没有“过时”的概念。 所以我构建了它——真正的衰变数学、真正的合并、真正的矛盾检测——然后将它与天真的方法进行基准测试，以证明它确实有效，而不是仅仅声称它有效。

这是在 Qwen Cloud 上花费大约两天时间构建的故事，包括损坏的部件。

实际机制

Synapse 存储的每个内存都会获得一个随时间变化的显着性分数：

salience(t) = importance_score * recall_boost(recall_count) * exp(-lambda * hours_since_last_recall)
recall_boost(n) = 1 + log(1 + n)
lambda = ln(2) / half_life_hours

进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

这里有两件事很重要。 首先，importance_score 不是一个关键字启发式方法——它是在写入时对 Qwen 的真正结构化输出调用，根据显式信号（“记住这一点”）、决策相关性（这是应该改变未来行为的事实吗？）和特异性对记忆进行评分。 其次，半衰期不是一个单一的全局常数。 情景细节——你在周二随机提到的——除非得到强化，否则会在大约 72 小时内消失。 语义事实——稳定的偏好，“我是素食主义者”——会在 30 天内衰减。 这种不对称就是重点：系统应该忘记你谈论的内容，而不是你告诉它的内容。

除了衰减之外，背景“睡眠通行证”还可以做两件事，这是简单的向量存储无法做到的：

  1. 巩固——将重复的片段提及（关于“从事我的黑客马拉松项目”的五个单独的消息）聚集到一个语义记忆中，淘汰原来的内容。
  2. 矛盾检测——当一个新事实直接取代一个旧事实时（你说你住在柏林，三周后你说你刚搬到里斯本），Qwen 会判断这对事实并淘汰陈旧的事实。 这就是简报所要求的“及时忘记过时的信息”。

在 Qwen Cloud 上构建

该项目中的每个 LLM 调用（聊天回复、重要性评分、内存提取、矛盾判断、聚类摘要，甚至基准测试自己的 LLM 作为评判评分）都会发送到真正的 Qwen Cloud 端点。 在任何地方都没有嘲笑的回应，包括在基准测试中。 这是我为自己设定的一条硬性规则：如果一种机制不能真正发挥作用，我会取消声明，而不是伪造输出。

到达那里并不是即时的。 Qwen Cloud 的 OpenAI 兼容端点是工作区特定的 — 不是您期望的通用主机，而是您必须在自己的控制台的 API 密钥页面上找到的 ws-<workspace-id>.<region>.maas.aliyuncs.com URL。 除此之外，qwen-max/qwen3.7-plus 处理聊天、评分、提取和合并，而 text-embedding-v3 处理用于检索和聚类的每个嵌入调用。

真正令我惊讶的一件事是：切换到更快的聊天模型 (qwen3.6-flash) 以加快基准测试运行速度时，偶尔会在结构化输出调用上返回格式错误的 JSON — 一个空的 {}，而真实的分数应该是这样的。 我没有用后备默认值来覆盖它，而是构建了一个重试包装器，专门重新提示形状故障，而不仅仅是网络故障：

def _chat_json_retrying(system_prompt, user_prompt, validate, temperature=0.2, max_attempts=3):
    for attempt in range(max_attempts):
        result = _chat_json(system_prompt, user_prompt, temperature)
        if validate(result):
            return result
    raise ValueError("Qwen returned an invalid shape after retries")

进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

虽然是一件小事，但这是默默产生垃圾数字的基准测试与当实际出现问题时大声失败的基准测试之间的区别。

几乎使整个基准测试无效的错误

这是我最诚实的一个。 合并过程应该比较时间戳，以找出两个相互矛盾的记忆中哪一个更新。 我运行了完整的基准测试——模拟 40 天的对话，进行了 110 次对话——使用通过代码传递的虚构现在值，这样我就可以将 40 天压缩到一个脚本运行中，而不是等待 40 天。

除了一个函数没有使用那个虚构的时间。 在决定哪个内存“较新”时，它会悄悄地回到 datetime.now() — 真正的挂钟时间。 模拟中的每条记忆，无论其虚构的对话日期如何，实际上都是在大致相同的真实时刻写入的。 因此，矛盾检查所依赖的时间戳比较是比较噪声，而不是实际模拟的时间顺序。

我通过在基准测试运行显示向后结果后直接查询数据库找到了它——系统自信地回答了一个过时的事实而不是当前的事实。 不是一个模糊的“准确性可以更好”，而是一个具体的、可追踪的错误。 我通过将模拟现在明确地线程化通过每个涉及时间戳的函数来修复它，而不是让任何东西默认为实时，并编写了一个回归测试来重现确切的失败：

def test_stale_fact_loses_even_when_inserted_after_correct_one():
    # The correct fact is "older" in simulated time but inserted into the DB
    # *after* the stale one — proving the fix isn't relying on insertion order.
    ...

进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

老实说，数字

我构建了第二个代理——相同的 Qwen 模型、相同的嵌入，唯一的区别是零衰减、零合并、零修剪——并通过相同的对话运行这两个代理。 真实结果：

  • 记忆数量：突触稳定在 117 个活跃记忆左右；朴素基线几乎线性增长到 220。
  • 每个查询的令牌成本：Synapse 在整个运行过程中保持在 130-170 范围内；随着指数的增长，初始基线飙升至 2000 个代币以上。
  • 回忆准确度：突触得分为 71%，而初始基线为 95%——而且我并没有隐瞒这个数字。

最后一个值得一坐。 我本可以精心挑选两个使项目看起来很棒的指标，然后将其留在那里。 相反，我深入研究了 Synapse 在原始回忆上失败的原因，并发现了两个具体的、可修复的原因：一个相似性预过滤门，它拒绝用不同单词表达的真正矛盾（相对于 0.75 的门，测量到的相似度低至 0.59）；以及一个重新排序公式，其中经常回忆的一般事实可能会超过强化程度较低但相关性更高的事实。 我修复了这两个问题，通过有针对性的回归测试和针对已部署应用程序的实时端到端测试验证了修复，并记录了所有这些 - 包括我没有时间在截止日期之前针对固定代码重新运行完整的多小时基准测试。

一张值得您信赖的图表比一张让您奉承的图表更有价值。

我会告诉开始这首歌的人

  • 如果你的记忆机制只在小范围内得到锻炼，你将无法发现真正的错误。 我的只有在 100 多个回合时才出现，此时时间戳和显着性实际上有分歧的空间。
  • 首先，或者至少并行地构建初始基线。 有一些可以比较的东西将“相信我，这很聪明”变成了一个数字。
  • 当长时间运行的工作中出现问题时，请抵制默默修补它的本能。 我的重试无效形状包装器和基准循环中的回滚并继续错误处理都来自于拒绝让一个错误的 LLM 响应导致两小时的运行中断。

Synapse 已在阿里云 ECS 上运行，代码是公开的并获得 MIT 许可，完整的诚实记录（包括错误、修复以及前后的数字）位于存储库中。

仓库：github.com/Boweii22/Synapse 现场演示：http://8.208.98.93 演示视频：youtu.be/0SXzcWqlZog

借助 Qwen Cloud 专为全球 AI 黑客马拉松系列的第 1 场 (MemoryAgent) 打造。
